note: Harness blind-spot for S5 (skipped in evaluationMode) + confirmed S5 silent-regression (demotes fused non-vector hits).

claim: NET-NEW (H/S): the harness CANNOT measure S5 — reorderTopNByCosine runs only in the else of if(evaluationMode) (hybrid-search.ts:1992 vs reorder :2023-2024; comment :2021-2022 'Skipped in evaluationMode'). A labeled-set eval passes evaluationMode:true -> exercises PRE-reorder order -> reports S5 a no-op. A/B searchFn MUST set evaluationMode:false + toggle SPECKIT_COSINE_TOPN_REORDER; metric nDCG@1 + top-1 precision.

EXTENDS A4 (H/S) — sharp A3 question answered YES: reorderTopNByCosine sorts by resolveAbsoluteRelevance (:2440) which returns real cosine ONLY for rows with .similarity, else falls back to RRF magnitude ~0.01-0.05 (types.ts:89-96). A hit RRF-ranked-high via fts/graph/bm25 but missed by the vector lane gets ~0.03 and sinks below every vector hit. cosine-low/RRF-high-but-right = the demoted class; root = A4 scale-split (memory-search.ts:493-494). Most-exposed: cross_document(29) + graph_relationship(12). Instrument: pre vs post-reorder rank, flag post>pre AND GT-relevant AND no .similarity.

EXTENDS A1 (H/M): S2 top-dominant verdict (assessRequestQuality confidence-scoring.ts:355-398, TOP_DOMINANT_THRESHOLD=0.8 :67) is doubly invisible — changes only good/weak/gap label AND called only in the formatter (search-results.ts:1056), never in the ablation searchFn; eval-db has no verdict table. Derive citability label from graded relevance (>=1 grade-3 in topK -> should-be good; hard_negative n=11 -> should-be gap); regression cell = false-good on hard_negatives (over-citing). Specifies labels + confusion cell A1 left abstract.

NET-NEW (M/M): S3 down-routes simple-tier (<=3 terms) to a channel-reduced path; only isLowSignalShortQuery escalates back (query-classifier.ts:157-166,245-248). Flag OFF -> all full-pipeline = recall ceiling. Golden set tags complexityTier (simple n=37). A/B recall@k on simple-tier, SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER on/off, partitioned {escalated,non-escalated}. MOST-LIKELY-NET-NEGATIVE: a precise 3-term query ('FSRS cold tier') is simple-tier, low stop-word, no trigger -> NOT escalated -> eats reduced route -> recall loss; heuristic can't rescue content-bearing short queries.
